Military Mar 30, 2026

Indonesia receives final A400M after rerouted delivery flight around Middle East

Indonesia has received its second and final Airbus A400M military transport aircraft, completing a two-aircraft order placed in…...

Indonesia's A400M delivery required Middle East rerouting, highlighting persistent geopolitical constraints affecting military aircraft movements in strategic transit regions.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

What Happens To Aircraft After Retirement?

The fate of retired commercial jets is a mystery to many, but in reality, there are many interesting journeys that they can go on.

Retired aircraft generate significant secondary-market value through conversion to cargo, charter, or parts harvesting, extending their economic lifecycle well beyond passenger service.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

The Striking Differences Between The Airbus A321XLR & Boeing 737 MAX 10

The Airbus A321XLR and Boeing 737 MAX 10 represent the pinnacle of evolution in the aviation industry, pushing narrowbodies to their absolute limits.

The A321XLR's 4,700nm range advantage over the 737 MAX 10 enables operators to bypass hub-and-spoke networks, fundamentally reshaping long-haul narrowbody economics.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Alaska Airlines Makes History: First-Ever Europe Flights Launch Next Month

The carrier is set to serve three European destinations this year.

Alaska Airlines' European expansion signals competitive repositioning against legacy carriers in transatlantic markets and potential network growth beyond current constraints.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Safety Mar 30, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Safety Management Systems succeed only when organizational culture incentivizes reporting near-misses without fear, transforming incidents into learning opportunities rather than punishment.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Safety Mar 29, 2026

Disruptive Passenger Forces American Airlines Boeing 737 MAX 8 To Divert To Detroit

Disruptive passenger forces American Airlines 737 MAX 8 diversion to Detroit, prompting FBI response and highlighting rising unruly behavior trends.

Rising unruly passenger incidents are creating operational costs through diversions while complicating MAX 8 fleet recovery efforts for carriers.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 29, 2026

Satellite imaging industry’s next challenge: getting systems to talk to each other

Executives say lack of incentives and interoperability slows ‘tipping and cueing’ across vendors...

Satellite imagery fragmentation is creating operational inefficiency in aerospace and defense applications where real-time data sharing between competing vendors remains economically misaligned.

See SpaceNews →

Safety Mar 29, 2026

Why Do Airlines Turn Off The Cabin Lights During Takeoff And Landing?

This practice ensures that passengers are better prepared during an emergency.

Dimmed cabin lighting accelerates passenger eye adaptation to darkness, enabling faster evacuation response during emergency evacuations on the ground.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Here’s How US Airline Economy Seat Pitch Compares In 2026

Even an extra inch of seat pitch can make all the difference to passenger comfort.

Most major U.S. carriers have converged on 31-32 inch economy pitch, with budget airlines remaining at 28-30 inches, creating a two-tier comfort standard that impacts fleet planning and competitive positioning.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Safety Mar 30, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation safety from investigating accidents after they occur to preventing them through systematic risk identification and mitigation before incidents happen.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

6 Commercial Aircraft With The Largest Wingspans Flying Today

The biggest wings in the sky in 2026.

Aircraft with maximum wingspans often trade operational flexibility for fuel efficiency and cargo capacity, reshaping airport infrastructure requirements globally.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 29, 2026

Chaos Continues: TSA Callouts Reach Record Numbers As Over 500 Officers Quit

The recent departure of over 500 TSA officers has left security checkpoints nationwide grappling with worsening delays.

TSA staffing crisis is creating cascading operational vulnerabilities that could compromise both security screening effectiveness and airport throughput during peak travel periods.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Why Many Airlines Choose Either Airbus Or Boeing

Both manufacturers have strengths, but there are benefits to standardization.

Airlines standardizing on a single manufacturer reduce pilot training costs, maintenance complexity, and supply chain vulnerabilities despite losing competitive leverage in negotiations.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Southwest Airlines Captain Flies Retirement Flight With First Officer Son

A Southwest captain’s final flight with his son highlights aviation family legacy, echoed by a similar emotional father-daughter story in Turkey.

Multi-generational cockpit crews are becoming more visible as legacy aviators retire, signaling both workforce continuity and the enduring appeal of aviation careers to industry families.

See Simple Flying →

▶20:47
Regulatory Mar 30, 2026

FAA Part 5 SMS for Small Operators: Safety Policy & Safety Promotion

This video provides an overview of implementing a safety management system (SMS), specifically aligned with FAA Part 5 regulations. Join us as we explore Blue Skies Charter, a fictional company, and learn about general SMS requirements, safety policy development and safety promotion strategies. This video covers essential components like leadership commitment, gap analysis, safety objectives, emergency response planning and employee training. Whether you're a small charter operator or interested in aviation safety, this video offers valuable insights into building an effective SMS.

Small charter operators can implement SMS cost-effectively by prioritizing leadership commitment, gap analysis, and employee training as foundational elements before expanding to complex safety programs.

See NBAA (YouTube) →

Military Mar 29, 2026

Misawa’s 13th Fighter Squadron Receives First F-35s

Misawa Air Base has received the U.S. Air Force’s first F-35A Lightning II fighters to be permanently based in Japan, beginning the transition from the F-16 to the fifth-gen aircraft. Misawa Air Base, home of the U.S. Air Force’s 35th Fighter Wing, has received its first four F-35A Lightning II...

Japan's first permanent F-35A deployment significantly enhances regional air superiority capabilities against evolving threats in the Indo-Pacific theater.

See The Aviationist →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Press Release: Aer Lingus takes off with Starlink inflight Wi-Fi

Aer Lingus has equipped the first aircraft in its fleet with Starlink, delivering ultrafast, reliable Wi-Fi to customers and marking a major step forward for customer experience. Customers travelling from Dublin to New York JFK on flight EI105 today are the first to experience the innovative...

Aer Lingus's Starlink integration enables real-time, satellite-based connectivity that bypasses traditional air-to-ground infrastructure limitations, reducing latency issues that have plagued previous inflight Wi-Fi systems.

See Runway Girl Network →

Drones Mar 29, 2026

Unidentified Drones Crash Near Kouvola, Finland, Authorities Investigate Possible Origin

Finnish authorities are investigating reports that one or two unmanned aerial systems havecrashed in southeastern...

The incident underscores growing border security vulnerabilities as drone operations near critical Nordic infrastructure remain difficult to attribute, potentially signaling escalated reconnaissance or testing activities in sensitive regions.

See sUAS News →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Op-Ed: Seating architecture must evolve to accommodate power, IFEC

Please enjoy this Op-Ed contribution from Unum VP Business Development, Alan McInnes Moving beyond the traditional considerations of structure, comfort and certification, aircraft seating is a critical integration point for power, in-flight entertainment (IFE) and connectivity systems that...

Modern aircraft seating must serve as a primary integration hub for power distribution and digital systems, fundamentally shifting design priorities beyond ergonomics and structural safety.

See Runway Girl Network →

▶15:03
Safety Mar 30, 2026

FAA Part 5 SMS for Small Operators: Safety Assurance

This video covers the safety management system (SMS) component safety assurance, which evaluates the continued effectiveness of implemented risk control strategies and supports the identification of new hazards. Whether you're an owner, operator or part of a small aviation team, this guide provides valuable insights and tools to enhance your safety management practices and ensure a safer operational environment.

Safety assurance in SMS requires continuous evaluation of control effectiveness and proactive hazard identification, not just compliance documentation.

See NBAA (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

India inaugurates Noida International Airport, Delhi’s second gateway

India opened the first phase of Noida International Airport (DXN) in Jewar, Uttar Pradesh, on March 28, 2026,…...

India's second Delhi airport reduces capacity constraints and positions the region to capture growing domestic and international traffic from South Asia's rapidly expanding aviation market.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 29, 2026

USAF E-3 Sentry AWACS damaged in Iranian strike on Saudi air base

An Iranian missile and drone attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia on March 27, 2026,…...

Loss of AWACS capability in the region creates critical gaps in air defense coordination across the Middle East during escalating tensions.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 29, 2026

‘Shots on goal and win the game’: NASA’s effort to accelerate lunar landings

While NASA outlined plans to increase the cadence of robotic lunar lander missions at a recent event, it said little about accelerating work on crewed lunar landers.

NASA's focus on accelerating robotic lunar missions masks a slower timeline for crewed lunar landers, creating a potential gap in the agency's moon exploration strategy.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 29, 2026

USAF A-10 strike on Iraqi military base kills seven soldiers

An airstrike attributed to a US Air Force A-10 Thunderbolt II hit a military clinic at the Habbaniyah…...

A-10 precision strike incidents highlight operational risks when close-air-support aircraft operate in complex threat environments with civilian-military infrastructure overlap.

See AeroTime Hub →

Industry Mar 30, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across the full aviation system—airlines, airports, and flight management—offers immediate efficiency gains that can accommodate projected traffic growth while reducing environmental impact before new aircraft technologies reach fleet scale.

See MDPI →

Military Mar 28, 2026

Northrop Grumman Testbed With AIM-260-Like Nose Spotted Flying Alongside Raytheon’s VOODOO 1 at Eglin AFB

The two testbeds, known by their callsigns SCAN 06 and VOODOO 1, carried out concurrent operations in Eglin’s highly instrumented airspace. For three consecutive days, on Mar. 24, 25 and 26, two testbed aircraft, the Northrop Grumman CRJ-700 carrying the U.S. registration code N806X and typically...

Concurrent testing of advanced air-to-air missile systems by competing defense contractors suggests accelerated development timelines for next-generation airborne weapons capabilities.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 28, 2026

Starfish Space finds a new partner for docking demonstration mission

Starfish Space has changed plans for a docking demonstration mission after its original partner backed out.

In-space servicing companies face commercial uncertainty as partners withdraw from missions, highlighting the need for long-term contractual commitments to validate orbital logistics capabilities.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 28, 2026

Germany Considering MQ-28A Ghost Bat CCA, Says Defense Minister

German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius said the MQ-28 Ghost Bat is among the uncrewed aircraft being evaluated to support the Eurofighter Typhoon fighter fleet. Germany’s defense minister Boris Pistorius said during his visit to Australia on Mar. 27, 2026, that Berlin is considering Boeing Defence...

Germany's evaluation of the MQ-28A Ghost Bat signals NATO's shift toward integrating autonomous combat aircraft with manned fighter fleets for enhanced operational capability.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 28, 2026

USMC F-35Cs and USS Tripoli ARG Arrive in the Middle East

A month after the start of the hostilities, the U.S. Marine Corps is increasing its presence in the Middle East with the arrival of a F-35C squadron and the USS Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group. Mar. 28, 2026, marks a month since the U.S. and Israel launched Operations Epic Fury and Roaring Lion,...

The F-35C carrier variant deployment demonstrates critical integration of advanced stealth capabilities within expeditionary amphibious operations for sustained Middle East presence.

See The Aviationist →

Drones Mar 28, 2026

Robinson Unmanned

Robinson Unmanned (formerly Ascent AeroSystems) is the uncrewed aircraft systems (UAS) business unit of Robinson...

Robinson's pivot to autonomous systems represents traditional helicopter manufacturers diversifying into growing UAS markets to offset rotorcraft demand volatility.

See sUAS News →

Drones Mar 28, 2026

Quantum Systems Strengthens Ukraine’s Air Defence Capabilities with 15,000 Interceptor Contract and WIY Drones Investment

In alignment with the defence priorities set out by the Ukrainian Government, Quantum Systems has...

Commercial drone manufacturers are pivoting to defence applications, signaling a structural shift in how unmanned systems integrate into modern military infrastructure.

See sUAS News →

Regulatory Mar 28, 2026

Singapore delays SAF levy rollout amid Middle East conflict

As the war in Iran continues to roil world oil markets, Singapore has delayed the rollout of its…...

Geopolitical instability affecting oil prices is forcing governments to defer aviation tax implementations that depend on fuel cost assumptions.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 28, 2026

Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC

The four Artemis 2 astronauts arrived in Florida March 27 for final preparations ahead of a launch still scheduled as soon as April 1.

With launch contingent on final vehicle checks and weather clearance, the arrival of the crew marks the final phase before NASA's return-to-Moon mission begins its critical countdown.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 27, 2026

[Updated] Iranian Attack on Prince Sultan Air Base Reportedly Struck U.S. KC-135s, E-3 Sentry

Satellite images show signs of an attack at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia, with The Wall Street Journal now reporting that an Iranian missile damaged “several U.S. refueling aircraft.” Satellite images emerged on Mar. 27, 2026, appeared to show signs of damage on an apron used by U.S....

Damage to KC-135 tankers reduces aerial refueling capacity for regional operations, potentially constraining U.S. Air Force sortie generation and extended-range mission capability.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

I Am Artemis: Erik Richards

Listen to this audio excerpt from Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager: For Erik Richards, supporting NASA’s first crewed Artemis mission to the Moon and back is the culmination of a career spent helping spacecraft communicate with Earth. Like many kids who grew up at the height of the...

The Near Space Network's communication infrastructure, managed by career specialists like Richards, represents a critical enabling capability that directly determines mission success for deep space exploration beyond Earth orbit.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon

NASA has awarded Intuitive Machines of Houston, $180.4 million to deliver NASA-funded science and technology to the lunar surface as part of the agency’s CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) initiative and Artemis program. This lunar delivery, which includes seven payloads — five of them NASA’s...

NASA's $180.4M investment in Intuitive Machines expands commercial lunar infrastructure beyond human missions, enabling private operators to monetize payload delivery services for sustained Artemis operations.

See NASA →

Indonesia receives final A400M after rerouted delivery flight around Middle East

Indonesia has received its second and final Airbus A400M military transport aircraft, completing a two-aircraft order placed in…...
